title: rubus × agrestis var. resepalus
intro: Rubus × agrestis var. resepalus, also known as wild bramble or European bramble, is a thorny shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × agrestis var. resepalus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and natural remedies.
